concept: The Circuit That Won't Close: the AI industry asked Alaska for 4,700 acres of military land for data centers, but Cook Inlet gas collapsed, GVEA burned diesel all winter, and the $120M turbine fix costs +195% since 2019 because the same AI buildout is buying every turbine. A living print-brutalist ledger on warm parchment that assembles, stamps, drains, and visibly refuses to balance; the price coil is drawn out of the demand glyphs and the final circuit carries the demand-to-price feedback arc.
style_mode: data-brutalist (print come alive: paper fiber, ink bleed, stamp impacts, foil vermilion)
voice: kokoro:af_heart (af_nicole planned but proved a slow whisper voice; af_heart grade A swapped in at build, still breaking the 3-run male streak)

fingerprint.palette: warm parchment field, carbon ink, hot vermilion signal, cold pewter
fingerprint.metaphor: the circuit that will not close, AI demand raised the price of the power it needs

palette: warm parchment field, carbon ink, hot vermilion signal, cold pewter
divergence_note: First light-ground print-world Dispatch: warm parchment and carbon ink where the last two were dark worlds (Yakutat's basalt territory map with a crimson seismic line, the sonar's amber echoes on abyssal black). The camera stands in schematic ledger space (exploded-diagram) from frame one: the map-open was cut after critique because it echoed Yakutat, so Alaska arrives as a pasted evidence clipping ON the ledger, not as a territory map. The subject is the data itself assembling (assemble-build), not a structure traced across a map (horizontal-traverse) or a swarm rising (vertical-rise). Layout is a hard modular ledger grid, not map-territory or a HUD bezel. Register is data-brutalist print, not editorial-schematic or instrument-readout. All 7 axes differ from both of the last 2 dispatches, and the spatial signature (exploded-diagram, grid-modular, assemble-build) appears nowhere in the last 4.
honest_caveat: Nothing is awarded, nothing is built, nothing is energized: drawn as a circuit whose breaker stays OPEN, every unbuilt element dotted and stamped PROPOSED (the loudest label), pulses dying at the gas gap twice, with the demand-to-price feedback arc showing WHY the loop resists closing. Closing type asks SHOULD ALASKA SAY YES?

shots.name: THE ASK
shots.centered: 4,700 ACRES stamps first in kinetic vermilion type; Alaska pastes in as an inked evidence clipping; three base stamps punch into the clipping; 12 solid-ink parcel chits tick in
shots.means: the ask, huge and concrete, on a ledger from frame one

shots.name: THE APPETITE
shots.centered: the 12 parcel chits multiply into a 100-house modular grid flooding with vermilion load; ~100,000 HOUSEHOLDS locks; the Hollister quote chip slams in at a tilt
shots.means: appetite quantified, and the neighbors get the loudest human line

shots.name: THE EMPTY PIPE
shots.centered: Cook Inlet gas band drains ABOUT 30% to UNDER 1% (LAST YEAR chit) beside a rising diesel-barrel stack AT LEAST 250,000 GALLONS A DAY (LAST WINTER, AT PEAKS chit)
shots.means: demand found an empty supply; the stopgap burned all winter

shots.name: THE TURBINE
shots.centered: the LM6000 draws itself points-to-faces; ~$120 MILLION stamps; the Shot-2 house glyphs stream back and chain into a coil strangling the machine, +195% SINCE 2019, cause slug THE BUYERS: DATA CENTERS EVERYWHERE
shots.means: the same appetite that knocked priced the fix; the coil is made of houses so the cause is visible muted

shots.name: THE OPEN CIRCUIT
shots.centered: the turbine shrinks into its node; the loop draws DEMAND-LAND-GENERATION-GAS with an open breaker gap plus a vermilion feedback arc DEMAND to price tag; pulses die at the gap; PROPOSED stamps loudest; closing type ALASKA HOLDS THE LAND, THE COLD, AND THE CHOICE then SHOULD ALASKA SAY YES?
shots.means: everything is real except the connection; the ending asks the reader to argue

shots.name: COLOPHON
shots.centered: a vermilion press-roller sweep; the ALASKA.AI wordmark stamps in with tagline and source colophon in staged beats
shots.means: the ledger is signed; the story stays open

beats.new_element: kinetic vermilion type stamps 4,700 ACRES with ink-bleed settle; eyebrow ALASKA.AI DISPATCH; sub-line THE AI INDUSTRY'S ASK OF ALASKA
Alaska evidence clipping pastes in with tape corners and torn edge; three base stamps punch in: JBER, EIELSON AFB, CLEAR SFS
beats.means: where the offer sits, as an exhibit on the ledger

beats.new_element: 12 solid-ink parcel chits tick in a strip; ink-slug print PROPOSALS CLOSED JUNE 29 (KTUU)
parcel chits multiply into a 100-house modular grid; vermilion load floods first rows; label ONE LARGE DATA CENTER
beats.transition: graphic-match: chit geometry carried into grid

beats.new_element: counter locks on ink slug = THE POWER OF ~100,000 HOUSEHOLDS (ADN); grid fully flooded
quote chip slams in at a tilt, reversed-out Hollister line, NEARBY RESIDENT, NORTH POLE (KTUU)
beats.means: the neighbors are in the film with the loudest human line

beats.new_element: the load line dives and becomes the top edge of a band labeled COOK INLET GAS, SHARE OF GVEA FUEL, ABOUT 30%
beats.means: the supply that used to be there

beats.new_element: the band drains to UNDER 1%; time chit LAST YEAR stamps
right panel: diesel barrels stack fast; counter AT LEAST 250,000 GALLONS A DAY; time chit LAST WINTER, AT PEAKS; frost creeps; GVEA plate sets as the barrels finish
beats.transition: split panel activates, plate folds in
beats.means: the stopgap at its peaks, and the utility at its limits

beats.new_element: LM6000 draws itself points-to-edges-to-faces; plate GE LM6000, 45 TO 58 MW
price stamp slams with paper shake: ~$120 MILLION

house glyphs stream back and chain into a coil strangling the machine; kinetic +195% SINCE 2019; cause slug THE BUYERS: DATA CENTERS EVERYWHERE, per Bloomberg, via the News-Miner; calendar chits WAITLISTS INTO THE EARLY 2030s
beats.transition: glyph stream + coil wrap; the music bed dies on the tightening creak
beats.means: the twist DRAWN: demand strangles the machine it wants

beats.new_element: turbine shrinks into a node; circuit draws as the music restarts: DEMAND solid, LAND solid, GENERATION dotted, GAS an open breaker gap; vermilion FEEDBACK ARC draws DEMAND to the price tag; ghost stamp PROPOSED loudest
beats.transition: carried-element: turbine becomes a node; hard audio seam resolves
beats.means: the honest caveat plus the self-feeding loop, as one picture

beats.new_element: pulses run the loop and die at the gap, twice; ink slugs NO AWARDS ANNOUNCED and JULY 13 GVEA EXECUTIVE SESSION
closing type stamps reversed-out: ALASKA HOLDS THE LAND, THE COLD, AND THE CHOICE. then SHOULD ALASKA SAY YES?
beats.means: the thesis lands and asks the reader to argue

beats.new_element: mask-wipe press-roller sweep; wordmark stamps, tagline, source colophon (af.mil, KTUU, ADN, News-Miner) in staged beats; paper drift and gentle fade to final frame

build_note: Shot times retimed at build to the MEASURED kokoro word timings (picture locked to voice, per Gate B critique): the ask 0-12.2, appetite 12.2-25.2, empty pipe 25.2-39.7, turbine 39.7-51.3 ($120M stamps on 'hundred twenty million' at 41.0, +195% on 'one hundred ninety five' at 45.9), open circuit 51.3-60.4 (question holds 2.6s), colophon 60.4-64. VO seg 9 re-recorded to 'turbine construction costs' for exact noun accuracy.
